decks.notes: The main concept of this deck revolves around utilizing various counterspells and disruption spells to protect your combo pieces, specifically Goblin Charbelcher. Through card draw and tutoring effects, the deck aims to assemble the necessary pieces to quickly generate a massive amount of mana and cast Goblin Charbelcher to deal lethal damage to the opponent in a single turn. By focusing on a landless strategy and prioritizing protection and card advantage, this deck aims to win the game through a powerful combo sequence with minimal reliance on traditional lands for mana production.

The deck focuses on assembling a variety of creatures with unique abilities to create powerful synergies and combos. By using cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Chord of Calling, the player can search for specific creatures to fit the situation and disrupt the opponent's game plan. With tools like Yawgmoth, Thran Physician and Walking Ballista, the deck can control the board and generate incremental advantage. The toolbox aspect allows for adaptability and versatility in different matchups, making it a flexible and strategic deck choice in Modern 1 vs 1.

This deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with key creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Griselbrand, then using cards like Goryo's Vengeance to bring them back into play for powerful and unexpected combos. The deck also includes control elements like Force of Negation and Thoughtseize to disrupt opponents while setting up its game plan. With a mix of instant reanimation and disruption, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with its powerful creatures and strategic plays.

This Ruby Storm deck focuses on quickly generating mana with ramp spells like Desperate Ritual and Manamorphose to cast multiple spells in one turn. It utilizes Ral, Monsoon Mage to deal damage to the opponent each time a spell is cast, ultimately leading to a lethal Grapeshot finish. The deck also includes Past in Flames to recur spells from the graveyard and Wrenn's Resolve to protect key combo pieces. The goal is to build up a high storm count and overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of spells for a decisive victory.

This deck focuses on disrupting and destroying opponents' lands while maintaining control through removal spells and creatures with flash. By using cards like Cleansing Wildfire and Field of Ruin, the deck aims to disrupt opponents' mana base and slow down their game plan. Erode and Wrath of the Skies provide additional removal options, while White Orchid Phantom and Solitude offer defensive creatures to stabilize the board. The inclusion of Price of Freedom and The Legend of Roku provides additional versatility and card advantage to outvalue opponents in the long run.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by ramping into powerful Eldrazi creatures while using cards like Devourer of Destiny and Disciple of Freyalise for mana acceleration. Emrakul, the Promised End is the powerhouse of the deck, while cards like Fight Rigging and Malevolent Rumble help control the board. Eldrazi Temple and Turntimber Symbiosis assist in getting the big Eldrazi out quickly. The deck also includes some protection with Kozilek's Command and Slumbering Trudge. Overall, the goal is to overwhelm the opponent with strong creatures and powerful control elements.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures and then reanimate them for a game-winning combo. Cards like Faithless Looting, Thoughtseize, and Thought Scour help to fill the graveyard, while Unearth and Persist allow for bringing back creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones to take control of the game. The combo potential is maximized with cards like Psychic Frog and Abhorrent Oculus, providing disruption and additional win conditions. Overall, this deck focuses on fast and efficient reanimation to overwhelm and defeat opponents in Modern 1 vs 1 matches.

This UW Control deck focuses on using counterspells, removal, and card advantage to control the game and outvalue the opponent. It aims to maintain board control, disrupt the opponent's game plan, and eventually win through card advantage and powerful finishers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Monumental Henge. The deck also includes Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion for additional synergy and value. Overall, this deck aims to control the game's pace, outmaneuver the opponent, and grind out a victory through superior card advantage and resource management.

This deck focuses on utilizing a combination of powerful spells and creatures to quickly overwhelm the opponent without the need for traditional land cards. By manipulating the top of the library with cards like Preordain and Tameshi, Reality Architect, the deck aims to quickly assemble the Goblin Charbelcher combo to deal massive amounts of damage. Control elements such as Suppression Ray and Disrupting Shoal help to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's strategy, while cards like Sea Gate Restoration provide card advantage to keep the engine running smoothly.

This deck focuses on casting powerful spells and creatures quickly to overwhelm the opponent. By utilizing cards that deal damage such as Tribal Flames and Lightning Bolt, combined with aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Scion of Draco, the deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total to zero. With a mix of land destruction and control spells like Stubborn Denial and Teferi, Time Raveler, this deck can maintain board presence and disrupt the opponent's strategy while pushing for a fast-paced victory.

This deck focuses on quickly reducing the opponent's life total using aggressive creatures like Goblin Guide and Monastery Swiftspear, supported by efficient burn spells such as Lightning Bolt, Rift Bolt, and Lava Spike. By consistently applying pressure with fast creatures and direct damage, the goal is to win the game before the opponent has a chance to stabilize. Cards like Boros Charm, Skullcrack, and Eidolon of the Great Revel help bypass opposing defenses and prevent life gain, ensuring a swift victory in the red deck's aggressive approach.

The main focus of this aggro Red Deck Wins deck is to consistently deal damage quickly and efficiently to defeat the opponent before they can set up their strategies. Using cards like Dragon's Rage Channeler, Monastery Swiftspear, and direct damage spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Spike, the deck aims to apply pressure from the very beginning. By utilizing low-cost spells and creatures to control the board and deal damage directly to the opponent, this deck aims to win the game through aggression and speed.

This deck focuses on tutoring for specific creatures using Chord of Calling, utilizing a toolbox approach to find the right creature for the situation. It also includes combo pieces like Cauldron Familiar and Gilded Goose to generate value and create a strong board presence. The inclusion of Yawgmoth, Thran Physician gives the deck additional versatility and options for removal. The deck also includes some disruptive cards like Abrupt Decay to deal with threats from the opponent. With a mix of creatures and combo pieces, this deck aims to outvalue and outpace the opponent.

The main concept of this Boros Ponza control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's resources with cards like Cleansing Wildfire, Wrath of the Skies, and Erode. The deck aims to generate card advantage and board control through efficient removal spells like Solitude and Path to Exile. By utilizing land destruction with cards like Field of Ruin and Demolition Field, the deck slows down the opponent's game plan while also ramping up its own mana production. The inclusion of cards like White Orchid Phantom and Price of Freedom provide additional value and flexibility in closing out the game.

The deck focuses on filling your graveyard with creatures using cards like Street Wraith and Generous Ent, then casting Living End to bring them all back onto the battlefield at once. This combo can quickly overwhelm your opponent with a board full of powerful creatures. The deck also includes disruption and filtering tools like Force of Negation and Subtlety to help control the game until you can execute your game-winning combo. This strategy aims to outpace your opponent and maintain board advantage through efficient resource management and clever card choices.

This deck focuses on assembling the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Mine, Urza's Tower, Urza's Power Plant) to generate a large amount of mana quickly. By using powerful Eldrazi creatures and control spells like Thought-Knot Seer and Karn, the Great Creator, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while deploying threats that overwhelm the opponent. The deck can also utilize artifacts like Mind Stone and Expedition Map to further enhance its mana production and consistency, leading to a strong late-game presence and potential for big plays.
